mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is an AI Gateway that allows you to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It provides features such as cost tracking, load balancing, and logging, making it suitable for applications requiring multiple LLM integrations. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential costs associated with API usage.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the LiteLLM CLI tool to manage your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5

```

> Install the specific version of LiteLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a new GKE cluster with the required version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to connect to the new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy LiteLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

> This command deploys LiteLLM using the provided YAML.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command sets up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> This command sets up monitoring.

uninstall.title: Remove LiteLLM Deployment
uninstall.description:
This will remove the LiteLLM deployment and all associated resources. Be aware of potential data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will delete the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get deployment litellm -o yaml > litellm-backup.yaml

kubectl set image deployment/litellm litellm=registry/image:1.81.14-stable.gpt-5.4-patch5

kubectl rollout status deployment/litellm

```

> Backup current deployment configuration.
> Upgrade to the new version.
> Verify that the upgrade was successful.
commands: kubectl get deployment litellm -o yaml > litellm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image does not exist or is not accessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and ensure all required environment variables are set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resources allocated to the nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to API'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM service is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the deployment status with 'kubectl get pods' and ensure the service is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM APIs and improved logging features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in a 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
